comment: Both concepts represent the highest-level, generic class encompassing all change operations within their respective ontologies, making them logically equivalent.
A BasicChange is a foundational, atomic change. This suggests it is a specialization of the general OntologicalChange, thus a subclass relationship.

A ComplexChange is a grouping of basic changes. This is conceptually similar to the OCH ChangeSet class, which groups multiple individual changes. They are sufficiently similar to be used interchangeably in many contexts.
Direct and exact lexical match for the operation of adding a class resource. They are defined as the same type of change.
Direct and exact lexical match for the operation of adding an instance (individual). They are defined as the same type of change.
Adding a label (rdfs:label) is a specific case of adding any annotation. Thus, the OCH entity (AddAnnotationToEntity) is a broader concept than the DynDiffOnto entity (addLabel).

Both object properties relate a change structure (action or set) to the resulting version of the ontology. The concepts are closely related and can be used interchangeably depending on the level of abstraction.
